title: Middle East crisis: For families, reunions add colour to festivities at Delhi airport
content: Families in Delhi are celebrating Holi and Eid with renewed joy after reuniting with relatives who returned from West Asia amid escalating tensions. Passengers arriving at IGI Airport expressed relief at being back in India, emphasizing the safety of their homeland after experiencing a tense but manageable situation abroad.

title: Kashmir protests over Khamenei’s killing: Meta blocks newspapers, MP booked for ‘misleading content’
content: Following protests over Iran's supreme leader's death, Jammu and Kashmir schools closed until March 7. Meta blocked local newspaper accounts, drawing criticism for censorship. Authorities registered cases against political figures for allegedly spreading misleading content, while the LG urged calm amidst ongoing demonstrations.

title: 'Ashes to ashes, dust to dust': Inside Masaan Holi at Manikarnika Ghat
content: Banaras' Masaan Holi offers a unique, contemplative celebration with sacred ashes from cremation pyres, unlike the riot of colours elsewhere. Rooted in Shaivite traditions, it symbolizes life's cyclical nature and confronts mortality. While social media has amplified its spectacle, the ritual's profound, existential encounter remains, urging immersion beyond filtered frames.

title: CEO Sam Altman to OpenAI staff: You don't get a say in how the military uses our AI
content: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman asserted employees have no say in US military operations, even after a Pentagon deal for classified AI deployment.  The move followed rival Anthropic's blacklisting, sparking internal and external criticism over timing and optics. Altman emphasized the Pentagon respects OpenAI's safety measures but retains operational control, while also acknowledging competitors might offer fewer restrictions.

title: US planning to arm Kurdish forces to trigger uprising in Iran: Report
content: The CIA is reportedly planning to arm Kurdish forces in Iran to incite a popular uprising, with the Trump administration in talks with opposition groups and Kurdish leaders. This move aims to draw Iranian security forces away from urban centers, potentially aiding civilian protests and straining Tehran's military resources.

title: OpenAI loses 1.5mn subscribers in less than 48 hours after Altman backs deal that Anthropic rejected
content: OpenAI faces a significant user exodus, with over 1.5 million reportedly leaving ChatGPT after agreeing to let the Pentagon use its AI on classified networks. This backlash, fueled by previous contracts and donations, has seen rival Anthropic's Claude chatbot surge in popularity. Users are now seeking ways to export their ChatGPT data and migrate to Claude.

title: India sets up control room, issues helpline number amid Middle East crisis
content: Amid escalating West Asia tensions, India's Ministry of External Affairs has established a special control room to assist its citizens residing in the region. The government is closely monitoring the evolving situation and remains in contact with regional governments and international partners to ensure the safety of nearly one crore Indians living there.

title: 'Dad, I want to be a cricketer’: How Daryl Mitchell chose cricket over rugby
content: Daryl Mitchell, son of former All Blacks player John Mitchell, found his passion for cricket despite a rugby upbringing. John encouraged Daryl's choices, leading him to excel in cricket.  Daryl's journey showcases dedication, embracing challenges, and a mature approach to success and failure, making him a vital player for the Black Caps.

title: Hotel, residential building hit: Israeli strikes kill 6 in Lebanon as Middle East crisis enters Day 5
content: Israeli air strikes have targeted several areas in Lebanon, including towns south of Beirut and a residential building in Baalbek, resulting in civilian casualties. Hezbollah, in turn, launched missiles at Israel's Haifa naval base. The Israeli military issued evacuation warnings for residents in southern Lebanon

title: Moving from ChatGPT to Gemini or Claude, make sure you do not make this big mistake
content: OpenAI's ChatGPT has seen a significant user decline following a Pentagon agreement. Millions have reportedly left the platform. Users considering switching to alternatives like Claude or Gemini are urged to back up their chat history. Deleting chats is possible but they are permanently removed from OpenAI systems within 30 days.

title: Bhojshala dispute: Historical records, ASI findings, full timeline and what comes next
content: The Bhojshala complex in Dhar, simultaneously known as a temple and mosque, remains a site of intense communal dispute. A recent 2,189-page ASI survey, intended to settle claims, has instead intensified the debate. The structure, built from reused temple materials, has seen centuries of conflicting religious use and legal battles.

title: 'I got Khamenei before he got me': Trump gives ten interviews to explain initiating ninth war
content: President Trump offered multiple justifications for the air campaign against Iran, including a preemptive strike against its supreme leader. He cited intelligence of assassination plots, election interference, and an imminent nuclear threat, though evidence remains unrevealed. Trump's strategy aims to control the narrative, presenting varied reasons to different outlets.

title: ‘We are never out of games’: Harry Brook sounds warning as England eye India upset
content: England captain Harry Brook believes his team thrives under pressure, highlighting their gritty journey to the T20 World Cup semifinals.  Despite a rocky start, they've shown character and calmness in close games. Brook backs his top order, including Jos Buttler, and praises Will Jacks' World Cup debut. He acknowledges the daunting home crowd but insists England is formidable.

title: From curry leaves to pineapples: War cripples Kerala’s Gulf-bound vegetable exports
content: Middle East crisis halts Kerala's vital vegetable exports to the Gulf, impacting nearly 70 exporters and thousands of farmers.  Daily shipments of around 90 tonnes, crucial for expatriate Keralites, have stopped due to mass flight cancellations.  This disruption, occurring during the peak Ramadan season, threatens to leave Gulf markets without familiar produce and causes significant financial losses.

title: Gold price prediction: What's the outlook for gold, silver amid Middle East tensions?
content: Gold price prediction today: Gold prices may face resistance at Rs 1,63,275, says Maneesh Sharma, AVP - Commodities & Currencies, Anand Rathi Shares and Stock Brokers.

title: 'Super pushy, followed us for 15 mins': Portuguese vlogger harassed in Mumbai
content: Two men were arrested by Agripada police for allegedly stalking and harassing a foreign tourist near Dhobi Ghat in Mahalaxmi. The digital content creator from Portugal posted a viral video of the incident, leading to a suo motu case and the apprehension of the accused.

title: India vs England: Can Abhishek Sharma find his fire at Wankhede?
content: Team India's Abhishek Sharma faces a crucial semifinal against England at the Wankhede, hoping to reignite his fearless batting form. Despite recent struggles against probing opposition tactics, the team management backs the young opener. Sharma is working on adjusting his game, particularly against spinners, aiming to replicate his past Wankhede success.

title: Battle of Equals: South Africa, New Zealand eye spot in T20 World Cup final
content: South Africa, the only unbeaten team, enter the T20 World Cup semi-final against New Zealand as favourites. Their balanced squad, with players stepping up consistently, and a strong pace attack, fuels their confidence.  New Zealand, coached by former SA mentor Rob Walter, aim to upset the Proteas' impressive run, acknowledging the need for their best performance.

title: US running out of missiles? Experts warn of shortages if Iran clashes continue for 10 days
content: US missile stockpiles could be depleted within 10 days if strikes on Iran continue, despite President Trump's claims of unlimited supplies. The Pentagon warns of high costs and risks associated with an extended campaign, particularly concerning precision munitions and advanced missile interceptors like THAAD.

title: 'Toxic' release postponed: Yash's film delayed by Middle East tensions; now out June 4
content: Kannada star Yash's ambitious film, 'Toxic: A Fairytale for Grown-Ups,' has been rescheduled to June 4, 2026. The postponement, attributed to Middle East tensions impacting global reach, ensures the movie avoids a clash with 'Dhurandhar 2.' This gangster action drama, co-written by Yash and Geetu Mohandas, is set in Goa and features a prominent female cast.

title: 'Most people we had in mind are dead': Trump after Khamenei assassination
content: US President Trump stated that "most of the people we had in mind are dead" following reported Iranian leadership casualties in Operation Epic Fury. He claimed a "third wave" of strikes has crippled Iran's capabilities, asserting the US acted preemptively to prevent an Iranian attack. Trump highlighted significant Iranian losses and a desire for immunity among remaining figures.

title: Israel strikes building where Iranian clerics were choosing Khamenei's successor
content: Israel reportedly conducted an airstrike on a building in Qom, Iran, where senior clerics of the Assembly of Experts were meeting to select a successor for the late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. Iran's state news agency disputed the claim, stating the targeted structure was unused.

title: Tamil Nadu polls: Congress wants 41, DMK sticks to 25 seats plus one Rajya Sabha
content: Congress and DMK commenced seat-sharing discussions for upcoming elections, with Congress seeking 41 assembly and two Rajya Sabha seats. DMK countered with an offer of 25 assembly seats and one Rajya Sabha seat, explicitly stating power-sharing is not on the agenda. Congress will revert after consulting Rahul Gandhi.

title: UAE stock market set to open today, 'uncertainty a major factor' for investors
content: The UAE is cautiously resuming normal activities, with capital markets set to reopen and businesses preparing for employees' return. Despite ongoing Iranian attacks and intercepted threats, authorities are bolstering flight capacity and reassuring the public. Food reserves remain adequate, though investor caution persists amid regional geopolitical tensions.

title: Rupee hit by Middle East crisis: Currency falls to new low of 92.15 against US Dollar
content: Rupee hit a record low of 92.15 against the US dollar, plummeting 66 paise amid escalating Middle East tensions and rising oil prices. Analysts predict continued currency strain as the conflict fuels inflation fears and impacts energy-importing economies like India.

title: Stock market crash today: Nifty50 drops over 2%; BSE Sensex plunges over 1,600 points
content: Stock market crash today: Nifty50 and BSE Sensex crashed in opening trade on Wednesday on rising Middle East tensions and escalating US-Israel-Iran war. While Nifty50 went below 24,400, BSE Sensex was down over 1,600 points.

title: Iran war worry for HP hoteliers: Will Israelis come this summer?
content: Hoteliers in Kullu Valley are anxiously monitoring the ongoing conflict, fearing a significant drop in Israeli tourist arrivals this summer.  These visitors are crucial to the region's hospitality industry, and a prolonged war could lead to substantial economic losses.  Currently, very few Israelis are present in the Kullu and Kangra districts.

title: A UP village with Khomeini roots mourns Khamenei
content: In Kintoor, India, residents mourn the recent killing of Iran's Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei, viewing his death as a personal loss. Khamenei's lineage traces back to Sayed Ahmad Musavi Hindi, a cleric who left Kintoor for Iran in 1834. Despite the centuries-old separation, the village feels a deep connection to the leader and his family.

title: Ayatollah’s son Mojtaba Khamenei emerges as front-runner to succeed slain Supreme Leader
content: Iran's Assembly of Experts is reportedly deliberating on Mojtaba Khamenei, son of the Supreme Leader, as his successor, with the IRGC pushing for his appointment. While Mojtaba is the front-runner, some clerics express reservations due to potential targeting by the US and Israel. The selection process is ongoing amid heightened tensions.

title: 'We've just begun': US says it has bombed over 2,000 targets in Iran
content: Israel and the United States have launched a major joint operation against Iran.  The campaign involves extensive strikes targeting Iranian military and nuclear facilities.  Iran has retaliated, hitting US diplomatic sites and energy infrastructure across the region.  The conflict has spread, causing casualties and displacement.  Global energy prices are rising as the Strait of Hormuz remains a point of tension.

title: Instinct over data: Inside India’s tactical shift at T20 World Cup 2026
content: From Jasprit Bumrah's introduction against Shimron Hetmyer to the angled plan for Sherfane Rutherford against West Indies in their virtual quarter-final, India have been a step ahead with their planning and have only gotten better with every game in the ongoing 2026 T20 World Cup.

title: Bullets rain in Haryana: Man pumps 8 shots into wife outside home
content: A man with a criminal record, facing three cases including murder, allegedly shot his wife dead outside their home in Jhajjar, Haryana. Police suspect a domestic dispute led to the fatal shooting of eight bullets. The accused, Sanjay, fled the scene and remains at large despite extensive manhunt operations. Investigations are ongoing, with authorities reviewing CCTV footage and conducting raids.

title: Asian stocks today: Korean stock markets hit circuit breaker after plunging 11%
content: Asian stock markets experienced a significant downturn on Wednesday.  Kospi led the decline, halting trading as war fears and rising oil prices unsettled investors.  Concerns over energy security overshadowed optimism in technology stocks.  Wall Street also saw a weak lead, reflecting unease about the expanding conflict.  High oil prices are a major worry for inflation and corporate earnings.

title: How Delhi cricket’s rejection shaped Sanju Samson’s resilience
content: Sanju Samson's career-defining 97* against West Indies in the T20 World Cup was followed by a candid admission of self-doubt.  Despite a stop-start journey, he learned from greats and found inner peace through a "mental reset," ultimately leading India to the semifinals after early setbacks.

title: Middle East on boil: Will US oil firms benefit from the heat?
content: Energy markets are reacting to Middle East conflict. US oil companies could see higher profits as prices climb. However, new investment in oil and gas production depends on how long these tensions last. Producers benefit from price surges. The market is watching for sustained price strength before committing to new projects.

title: Loan-recovery agent steps on man’s foot while eating dosa in Bengaluru hotel, murdered
content: A tragic incident unfolded in Basaveshwaranagar where a loan-recovery agent, Shashikumar, was fatally stabbed after an argument over accidentally stepping on another man's foot while eating dosa. The dispute escalated when the accused, Manjunath and his brother Bhagyaraj, allegedly assaulted and killed Shashikumar. He leaves behind his parents, wife, and an infant child.

title: 'Not happy with UK either': Trump slams European nations, singles out Spain over Iran campaign
content: US President Trump has sharply criticized European nations, particularly Spain, for their stance on the US-Israel military campaign against Iran. While Germany is praised, Spain's refusal to allow base usage for operations against Iran has drawn Trump's ire. The conflict escalates with regional attacks and rising oil prices.

title: Jasprit Bumrah - The 'different' boy who never gave up
content: Jasprit Bumrah's remarkable journey from an illness-plagued Ranji Trophy match to becoming India's premier fast bowler is a testament to his resilience. Discovered by John Wright for Mumbai Indians, Bumrah's unique action and yorkers impressed even Sachin Tendulkar. Despite injury setbacks, he consistently delivers impactful performances, proving critics wrong and solidifying his status as a polished diamond in Indian cricket.

title: Farmer in Kerala’s Wayanad dies in suspected elephant attack
content: According to local residents, the victim had gone to drive off an elephant that had entered his farm. However, as he did not return, they checked and found him lying on the ground with elephant footprints around him

title: T.N. CM Stalin slams BJP govt. at Centre for use of Hindi names in Tamil, English scripts
content: Taking strong objection to the practice of writing Hindi names even in Tamil and English instead of using appropriate terms in the respective languages, Mr. Stalin, in a post on X, described the practice as “one language – three scripts” and said it was a form of Hindi imposition.

title: Rajya Sabha tangle: Negotiation continues in MVA, BJP issues list of four candidates
content: State Congress leadership wants the Rajya Sabha seat allocated to itself, but several leaders The Hindu spoke with, said they might have to support Sharad Pawar if he decides to contest

title: Masi Maham and Valli Kalyanam festivities light up temples
content: As the Maham star reigned for two days, Maasi Maham was celebrated at many temples on Monday (March 2) and Tuesday (March 3), when the utsava idols were taken to the sea for a ceremonial dip

title: Govt closely monitoring West Asia turmoil; safety mechanisms activated for seafarers: Sonowal
content: Sonowal said his Ministry is prepared to extend all necessary operational, diplomatic and humanitarian support to safeguard Indian vessels and personnel

title: The grammar of belonging in Barpeta’s Dol Jatra
content: Barpeta’s Dol Jatra reveals how theology, ecology and shared labour can reimagine community in an age of fragmentation.

title: West Asia crisis: MEA establishes control room to assist Indians
content: The Control Room can be contacted from 9 a.m. to 9 p.m. at: 1800118797 (Toll Free) +91 11 2301 2113, +91 11 2301 4104, +91 11 2301 7905

title: South Western Railway registers 14.31% growth, records overall gross revenue of ₹8,550 crore
content: Freight revenue reached ₹4,876 crore during the year, reflecting a significant growth of 18.27%, while passenger revenue increased to ₹3,154 crore, registering a growth of 9.25%

title: Pahalgam terror attack: NIA seeks China’s legal assistance to trace information on GoPro linked to attack
content: According to the application, the NIA, during the course of the investigation into the case, seized various material objects and electronic devices connected with the conspiracy and execution of the terrorist attack

title: Sabarimala gold case: Kerala court grants statutory bail to former TDB president A. Padmakumar
content: Having already secured bail in kattilappali (doorframe) case, the latest bail clears way for Padmakumar’s release from prison

title: Sharing video or photo of minor girl in Mallikarjun Mutya POCSO case will attract legal action by Yadgir police in Karnataka
content: Superintendent of Police Prutwik Shankar stated that sharing and uploading information or photos or videos of the minor girl, and identifying her parents by posting their photos, is illegal

title: EmpowHer 1.0 to help over 6,000 women entrepreneurs in Tamil Nadu
content: The inaugural cohort comprises 160 aspiring women entrepreneurs from Chennai, Kancheepuram, Chengalpattu, and Tiruvallur

title: Kerala private sector nurses go on strike; services at 490 hospitals and clinics hit
content: They seek higher pay, improved staffing levels to reduce workload, better patient-caregiver ratio and end to alleged arbitrary lay-offs

title: Centre sanctions ₹3,942 crore to boost digital connectivity in Chhattisgarh: CM Vishnu Deo Sai
content: As part of the ambitious project, 11,682 gram panchayats in the State will be connected through a “ring topology-based” network

title: KLETU team bags 2nd rank at national-level m-BAJA SAE India 2026 in Hyderabad
content: KLE Technological University’s ‘Team Euros Racing’ has secured All India Rank 2 at the m-BAJA SAE India 2026 competition held at BVRIT, Narsapur (Hyderabad, Telangana) from February 25 to March 1

title: TVK supporters target media outlets online, draw ire
content: They created several fake social media accounts in the names of media outlets, as well as many fictitious media organisations, and indulged in spreading false and defamatory remarks targeting journalists, Chennai Press Club said in a statement

title: Protests against Khamenei's killing: Curbs remain in force in Kashmir
content: As a precautionary measure, the government shut educational institutions till March 7, while mobile internet speed continued to remain throttled

title: Five from Bengaluru killed in a road accident in Chittoor
content: The car in which they were travelling rammed the rear of a stationary lorry on the highway. The impact of the collision was severe, resulting in the death of all five occupants

title: Trail of deadly blasts in Maharashtra’s Nagpur; former Home Minister demands safety audits
content: At least 10 factories in Nagpur’s Bazargaon area — including Solar Explosives Industries Ltd., Chamundi Explosives, and Asian Fire Works — that are explosives factories have been plagued by frequent blasts, the Anil Deshmukh said.

title: ‘Toxic’ postponed: Yash-starrer delayed due to “Middle East tensions”
content: ‘Toxic’, starring Yash and directed by Geetu Mohandas, was scheduled to hit the screens on March 19, 2026. The film is jointly produced by KVN Productions and Monster Mind Creations

title: Ex-Navy official arrested with 1.2 kg of ganja, cannabis plants in Kerala
content: The accused, identified as Binu V. William, was nabbed during a search conducted at his residence in Poomkavu based on a tip-off

title: Srinagar MP, ex-Mayor booked for circulating false content on social media
content: The FIR against Aga Ruhullah and Junaid Azim Matoo is egregiously unwarranted and unjust; must be withdrawn immediately, said Mehbooba Mufti

title: Minister tells officials to fill Hanchinal lake in Vijayapura with treated water
content: The Minister told officials to ensure that all feeding canals were cleared of encroachments to remove impediments to water flow to the lake

title: Delhi Police on high alert for Holi; 134 intersections under strict watch
content: A senior police officer said teams have been deployed at 134 major intersections to nab drunken driving culprits. Serious violation will invite suspension of driving licence for a minimum of three months

title: DMK to hold seat-sharing talks with Kamal Haasan‘s MNM on March 4
content: As the DMK had previously alloted a Rajya Sabha seat to Mr. Haasan, his party may be given only one Assembly seat

title: Jairam Ramesh flays BJP MPs' praise of Modi, compares Parliament to North Korea's Assembly
content: The Congress leader called it “unfortunate” that the Modi government is “totally allied with Israel” and “afraid of Trump”

title: Silent surge in districts: door-to-door screening reveals uneven burden of NCDs in Karnataka
content: A senior official said the district-level variations underline the need for targeted public health strategies rather than a uniform approach

title: Education: Strong foundation, steady expansion, and some striking signals
content: Several deemed universities have evolved into global hubs, attracting students and faculty from across India and abroad, with cutting-edge infrastructure and laboratories rivalling those of national institutions.

title: Massive blaze guts 15 furniture sheds in Bachupally; no casualties reported
content: Telangana Fire Department personnel were engaged in firefighting operations at furniture sheds in Bachupally of Hyderabad to ensure embers do not reignite

title: Party is bigger than individuals, says Congress MP Manickam Tagore who advocated power-sharing with DMK
content: In a message on social media, Mr. Tagore emphasised the importance of discipline and unity within the organisation

title: Congress to seal seat-sharing pact with DMK on March 4 in significant breakthrough in negotiations
content: Rahul Gandhi has reportedly conveyed his approval to Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge to go ahead with the deal

title: On India’s fighter jet acquisitions
content: The procurement of the Rafale jets is not just fleet expansion. It is a structural shift in how India positions itself within global defence hierarchies. Securing access to mission software and autonomy to integrate weapons in fighter aircraft is central to advancing India’s efforts to indigenise defence development.

title: Finland’s President Stubb kicks off four-day visit to India; trade, innovation top agenda
content: The visit, featuring a high-level delegation of Ministers, officials, and business leaders, aims to strengthen bilateral ties and expand cooperation between India and Finland across various sectors

title: This Assam startup makes sustainable paper from water hyacinth
content: Wildlife enthusiasts Aniket Dhar and Rupankar Bhattacharjee turn an invasive aquatic weed into chemical-free paper while supporting local livelihoods

title: Why India needs to radically think its doctoral education programmes
content: India faces many real-world problems that could benefit from high-quality PhD research. These issues span public health, agriculture, sustainability, digital inclusion, and education. The question is whether our current systems support and encourage studies grounded in what the people need

title: Orchid Park added to Kaziranga’s attractions in Assam
content: Assam Chief Minister inaugurated the park on a day a Sikkim specialist announced a hybrid orchid named after the prime rhino habitat

title: Bihar CM Nitish Kumar’s son likely to join active politics soon: JD(U) leaders
content: Posters of Nishant Kumar demanding him to join active politics too had earlier adorned walls of the JD-U state headquarters in Patna

title: Trinamool cries foul over tea garden workers struck off  electoral rolls or kept in adjudication list
content: In January, the ECI allowed records of Tea Gardens and Cinchona plantations as documents for SIR 2026

title: Congress MP Imran Masood meets representative of Iran’s late Supreme leader, offers condolences
content: Mr. Masood described the death of the Iranian leader Khamenei as a “sad event” and personally conveyed his sympathies during the meeting

title: The 1995 caste reprisals that scarred southern T.N. also touched Communist leader Nallakannu’s life
content: On December 3, 1995, Nallakannu’s 84-year-old father-in-law, A.K. Annasamy, was murdered by a gang while he was asleep at his house in Marudanvalvu village, then part of Chidambaranar district (now Thoothukudi district)

title: Tamil Nadu’s saga of controversial Governors begins with Sri Prakasa of the 1950s
content: When the talk of C. Rajagopalachari becoming the Chief Minister again began doing rounds, his associate and prominent Tamil journalist-writer ‘Kalki’ R. Krishnamurthy likened it in his Tamil weekly to Ramana Maharishi becoming chairman of Tiruvannamalai municipality.

title: Over 1,000 entries received for The Hindu Margazhi Music Contest
content: The video links have been sent to the judges, who watch the presentations and select the winners based on marks

title: J&K Police lodge FIR against Kashmir MP Ruhullah, former Srinagar mayor Mattu
content: Junaid Azim Mattu said in a Kashmir today fellow mainstream leaders have “surrendered and sold their tongues to the BJP-led Government and chosen to sound shamefully diplomatic over the assassination of Khamenei and the brutal war on Iran and Palestine”.

title: Madras High Court grants bail to woman accused of sponsoring BSP leader Armstrong’s murder
content: Justice K. Rajasekar however denies bail to an advocate, cancels the bail granted by a sessions court to 12 other co-accused and confirms the bail granted to two more of the 30 accused persons

title: TVK yet to transform into political party: Karti Chidambaram
content: He adds that actor’s Vijay’s outfit still operated like a “fan club”  and doesn’t have “a command and control structure”

title: Stalin thanks Siddaramaiah for Karnataka’s support to call for renewed national conversation on Union-State relations
content: In a communication dated March 2, he wrote to the T.N. Chief Minister expressing Karnataka’s strong support on the issue

title: Indian airlines plan to operate 58 flights to West Asia on March 4: Govt
content: "Indian carriers are further planning 58 flights on 4th March, including 30 by IndiGo and 23 by Air India and Air India Express," Civil Aviation Ministry said.

title: Siddaramaiah rubbishes allegations of spying on D.K. Shivakumar loyalists
content: Chief Minister Siddaramaiah rubbished allegations of spying and phone tapping made by Leader of Opposition in the Assembly R. Ashok and JD(S) leader and Union Minister for Heavy Industries and Steel H.D. Kumaraswamy

title: Under pressure from the Dalit Left, recruitment GO likely to come up for discussion again in Karnataka Cabinet
content: Dalit left community delegation, including Ministers K.H. Muniyappa and R.B. Thimmapur, met Chief Minister Siddaramaiah on Tuesday and is learnt to have exerted pressure for withdrawal of GO on recruitment

title: D.K. Shivakumar urges Karnataka MPs to put pressure on Centre to seek approval to raise Almatti dam height
content: Andhra Pradesh government has written to the Centre not to approve the proposal to raise the dam height to 524.26 metres

title: Canada PM's visit to India built strong foundation for truly renewed partnership: Modi
content: The Canadian Prime Minister was in India from February 27 to March 2, during which India and Canada sealed key pacts on supplies of Uranium and critical minerals and agreed to conclude a comprehensive economic partnership agreement soon.

title: Karnataka HC directs State government to formulate policy for accurate content management on govt. websites
content: Justice Suraj Govindaraj has issued the directions while dismissing a petition filed in 2016 by Jain International Residential School raising certain disputes over correctness of levy of water charges

title: IBC represents legislative choice to privilege speed over exhaustive judicial scrutiny: Supreme Court judgment
content: ‘Courts need to remain vigilant against any temptation to expand the scope of review beyond the narrow boundaries prescribed by the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code,’ the judgment says

title: Gaurav Gogoi among 42 candidates in Congress first list for Assam polls
content: Five sitting MLAs have been re-nominated, while former State party president Ripun Borah makes a comeback

title: Govt. moves to help stranded Telugus in Bahrain, other Gulf nations
content: Chief Minister Chandrababu Naidu directs APNRTS to ensure uninterrupted food supply and all possible support to expatriates facing hardship, says APNRTS president Ravi Kumar Vemuru

title: Mamata stresses on secularism; Suvendu raises Hindutva pitch
content: Bengal CM says it is not the policy of her government to divide and discriminate; BJP leader says “words like secularism are being misinterpreted” in the country

title: Saba Shawl becomes first Kashmiri woman Superintendent of Srinagar Central Jail
content: She has already served in the administration department at the Central Jail in Kot Balwal, Jammu, and the Kupwara Jail.

title: Congress, DMK stand their ground, fail to seal pact
content: Sources said the DMK raised its offer from 25 to 27 Assembly seats and one Rajya Sabha seat, while the Congress leadership is demanding 30 Assembly seats and a Rajya Sabha seat.

title: Meta takes down social media handles of prominent Jammu and Kashmir news outlets
content: Such actions not only silence legitimate voices but also undermine the fundamental right to freedom of expression, says Mehbooba

title: Rural jobs scheme workers flag glitches in monitoring app
content: Workers’ union Mazdoor Kisan Shakti Sangathan reports widespread failures in National Mobile Monitoring System’s facial recognition update; government officials claim that over 22 lakh workers successfully recorded their attendance using the new facial recognition feature

title: Manipur CM flags off multi-community national integration tour
content: Twenty-four youths from Meitei, Kuki, Naga and Pangal (Muslim) communities to travel across five cities in first such initiative since 2023 ethnic violence

title: Left parties slam government’s silence on Iran attack, Khamenei killing
content: CPI(M) leader Brinda Karat says Prime Minister Narendra Modi has “bowed” in front of U.S. President Donald Trump and has held Israel Prime Minister “Benjamin Netanyahu’s bloodied hands”

title: BJP leader Ravi Shankar Prasad to head Lok Sabha’s Privileges Committee
content: The panel examines cases referred by the Speaker and submits reports on matters where breaches of privilege are alleged

title: Boy lynched over suspicion of stealing goats in Odisha
content: When the victims were passing by motor cycle, they were caught hold of and asked about stolen goats, police  said; the two victims were then then thrashed with sticks

title: Men's Bubble Watch: Georgia, Auburn notch key wins on opposite ends of SEC bubble
content: The Bulldogs became an effective NCAA tournament 'lock' while the Tigers stopped their nosedive with victories.

title: Men's Bracketology: Miami (Ohio) or Auburn? Sorry, Bruce Pearl -- it's the RedHawks
content: The RedHawks are two wins away from an undefeated regular season while the Tigers are barely above .500.

title: Prosecutors to offer plea agreements in poker case
content: Prosecutors said on Tuesday that they expect to extend formal plea agreements to 12 of the 31 defendants in the federal case into rigged poker games involving Chauncey Billups and Damon Jones.

title: Redick calls sideline tiff with Luka 'very normal'
content: Lakers coach JJ Redick called his testy bench exchange with Luka Doncic over the weekend much ado about nothing, saying he didn't understand why it went viral.

title: Wynn leaves Lady Vols; senior day 'breaking point'
content: Kaiya Wynn, a reserve guard on the Tennessee women's basketball team, said Tuesday she is leaving the program, calling what she felt was a slight on senior day this past weekend a "breaking point."

title: Sources: Cardinals to cut QB Murray next week
content: The Cardinals informed quarterback Kyler Murray that they intend to release him on the first day of the league year next Wednesday, barring a trade between now and then, sources told ESPN's Adam Schefter on Monday.

title: Miami (Ohio) extends winning streak to 30 games
content: No. 19 Miami (Ohio) closed within a victory of a perfect regular season with a 74-72 win over Toledo on Tuesday night that extended the RedHawks' season-opening winning streak to 30 games.

title: Colts place rarely used transition tag on QB Jones
content: The Colts place the rarely used transition tag on QB Daniel Jones, preserving their right to match any offer sheet in free agency.
